scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap clearly identified (300/min vs 15-20/min with ~280 msgs/min growth), unbounded queue growth described with concrete projections (16,800 messages in 1 hour), visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with detailed arithmetic (batch of 10 at ~3s each = ~30s vs 30s timeout), missing DLQ identified as a blocker, queue depth metric gap called out as critical, autoscaling recommended as primary solution with parallelism as secondary, verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput math is precise and includes concrete projections (280 msgs/min growth, 16,800 backlog in 1 hour, 4-day SQS retention)
Visibility timeout analysis is exceptionally detailed — identifies the race condition arithmetic and provides specific remediation (90-120s timeout, per-message ChangeMessageVisibility)
DLQ absence correctly framed as a blocker with clear explanation of poison message impact on already-starved capacity
Queue depth metric gap explicitly called CRITICAL and identified as the single most important missing metric
Autoscaling recommended as first-choice solution with fallback options (concurrent processing, defined SLA degradation)
Goes well beyond rubric requirements — identifies idempotency gaps, OOM risks, missing per-job timeouts, downstream dependency failures, and graceful degradation gaps
Prioritized recommendations table clearly separates blockers from should-haves and nice-to-haves
Escalation routing is appropriate — architecture decisions to MJ, domain correctness to Bird
